name: Social Encounter: Beat 12 — Rickety Frets (The Three Demands)

text.content:
Social Encounter: Beat 12 — Rickety Frets (The Three Demands)

Act: Act 2

Setup

The party has found the Bioluminescent Library — 100ft across, every inch carved with Root-Runes. At the center, Rickety Frets is waiting. They are a spirit-shade: very tall, very thin, cadaverous in faded paper-colored robes, ink-stained hands. They introduce themselves: "I am Svellheim's historian when there was a Svellheim worth chronicling. Now I chronicle its ending."

Rickety Frets will not simply share information. They have been alone for too long, watching Svellheim die, and they have three demands that must be sworn before they help. The negotiation begins with their questions: What year is it? How is the world dying? Is there still a sky?

This is not a hostile negotiation — Frets wants to help. They simply need to know the party will honor their legacy before they trust them. Interest starts at 3 because they are already inclined to help anyone heading toward the Emberwell.

Rickety Frets

Social Stats

  • Starting Attitude: Indifferent
  • Persuasion DC: 15
  • Insight DC: 13 (to read motivations/pitfalls)

Motivations (Appeal to These)

  • Being remembered — not just their name, but that someone stayed and kept the record: If the PCs appeal to this, they gain advantage on their next Persuasion or Deception check, or the DC is reduced by 2.
  • The survival of Svellheim's historical record (the library and its contents): If the PCs appeal to this, they gain advantage on their next Persuasion or Deception check, or the DC is reduced by 2.
  • The Bundingsteinn being protected or destroyed rather than falling to Grafvitnir: If the PCs appeal to this, they gain advantage on their next Persuasion or Deception check, or the DC is reduced by 2.
  • Someone returning to report what happened after — closure, whatever form it takes: If the PCs appeal to this, they gain advantage on their next Persuasion or Deception check, or the DC is reduced by 2.

Pitfalls (Avoid These)

  • Dismissing or minimizing what they've done ("just" a historian, "just" waited): If the PCs trigger this, they have disadvantage on their next check, or the DC increases by 2.
  • Treating the Three Demands as negotiable or asking to reduce them: If the PCs trigger this, they have disadvantage on their next check, or the DC increases by 2.
  • Expressing certainty that Svellheim will be restored (they've heard too many promises): If the PCs trigger this, they have disadvantage on their next check, or the DC increases by 2.
  • Rushing them or showing impatience with their questions about the outside world: If the PCs trigger this, they have disadvantage on their next check, or the DC increases by 2.

Running the Social Encounter

This social encounter uses a simple attitude track. The NPC starts at a given attitude. The PCs make Charisma checks (Persuasion, Deception, or Intimidation) against the listed DC to shift the NPC's attitude one step toward Helpful. Three shifts to Helpful = success. Dropping to Hostile = failure.

Key Skills: Persuasion, Deception, Intimidation, Insight (to discover motivations/pitfalls)

Each PC can attempt one check per round. A round represents roughly 1-2 minutes of in-character conversation.

Outcomes

Full Success (Interest reaches 5): Rickety Frets swears themselves to the party's cause within the limits of their anchor. They share everything: the route through the Bone-Fields, the warning about the boundary thinness there, the Bundingsteinn's theft by Wyrd-Draugr 3 weeks ago, and the key revelation — "The Keepers didn't keep the flame. They became the flame." They also provide the Three Demands oath text and provisions from the library (including the Bioluminescent Lantern). Kaelen enters Layer 2.

Partial Success (Interest 3–4 at Patience 0): Rickety Frets shares the route and the basic warning but not the deeper lore about the Ever-Ember rite. The Bundingsteinn theft is mentioned but without the Wyrd-Draugr detail. The Three Demands are still sworn — Frets needs this too much to withhold it entirely.

Failure (Interest reaches 0): Rickety Frets withdraws. "You are not what I hoped. Go. I will not stop you — I simply will not help you." The party gets no lore, no route information, no Demands. They may attempt the route uninformed (harder navigation) and miss the key revelation about the Keepers.
